title: Creamy Sweet Indian Paneer Curry
description: A festive, sour‑sweet paneer curry with a luscious creamy base, finished with a fragrant tadka of mustard seeds, cumin and curry leaves.
instructions: Heat the oil in a heavy‑bottomed pan over medium heat.
Add cumin seeds, mustard seeds and curry leaves; let them splutter for 10–15 seconds.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until golden brown, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the tomato puree, garam masala, red chili powder and a pinch of salt; cook for 4 minutes until the mixture thickens.
Whisk in the yogurt and ground sugar until smooth; reduce heat to low and simmer for 3 minutes.
Add the paneer cubes and tamarind pulp, gently folding to coat; cook for 5 minutes, allowing flavors to meld.
Pour in the fresh cream and water, stirring gently; simmer for another 3 minutes until the gravy reaches desired consistency.
Season with salt to taste, then remove from heat.
Sprinkle chopped coriander leaves on top and serve hot with steamed rice or naan.
Optional: drizzle a little more fresh cream or a pinch of sugar for added richness before serving.
